constraints: Do not reintroduce the Ralph or enterprise master-plan file here.
This branch is the probate outbound + lease-option inbound MVP.
Live Supabase schema application, backend cutover, persistence wiring, and migration application are deferred.
Keep probate outbound and lease-option inbound separate in state, routing, and Mission Control surfaces.
Keep execution memory-backed and fixture-backed for this pass.
Providers are transport only. Ares is the deterministic runtime. Hermes is the browser/research driver.

deferredStories.title: Keep the lead-machine and opportunities Supabase migration blueprint dormant
deferredStories.reason: The branch explicitly defers live Supabase schema work and migration application in this pass.

deferredStories.title: Keep the dedicated lead-machine Supabase adapter dormant and memory-default
deferredStories.reason: The branch explicitly defers backend persistence wiring and live Supabase cutover in this pass.

userStories.title: Bring the probate lead-machine slice into feature/loose-ends
userStories.goal: Port the probate outbound / lead-machine domain slice from origin/main into this branch without activating live Supabase wiring.
userStories.acceptanceGate: The current branch has the newer Ares probate and lead-machine services locally, still runnable in memory mode.
tasks: Compare the current branch against origin/main and identify the exact lead-machine files that already exist upstream.
Port models first, then repositories, then services, then API-adjacent wiring so the branch stays runnable at each step.
Prefer cherry-picking or file-porting over rewriting logic that already exists upstream.
Keep all imported paths behind repository abstractions and do not switch on the deferred Supabase backend path.
Port or adapt the matching tests next to the code they cover so each imported batch has a verification target.
Run the imported lead-machine tests in memory mode before moving to the probate write path story.
verification: git diff origin/main -- app/models app/db app/services app/api tests

userStories.title: Build the probate outbound write path
userStories.goal: Ship the probate intake, enqueue, and Instantly webhook paths as replay-safe in-memory runtime flows.
userStories.acceptanceGate: Hermes can hand Ares probate records, Ares can enqueue them into Instantly, and replies, suppressions, and tasks come back into Ares through a replay-safe path.
tasks: Add and mount the new lead-machine API router without disturbing the existing marketing routes.
Write and pass failing tests for POST /lead-machine/probate/intake first.
Write and pass failing tests for POST /lead-machine/outbound/enqueue second.
Write and pass failing tests for POST /lead-machine/webhooks/instantly third.
Persist provider receipt first, then append canonical lead events, update lead state, apply suppressions, and create tasks.
Add Trigger fan-out only where durable async work is actually needed, and keep the backend memory-backed.

userStories.title: Harden the lease-option inbound lane
userStories.goal: Make the existing submit, booking, SMS, and non-booker sequence flow idempotent, replay-safe, and timeline-complete without changing product scope.
userStories.acceptanceGate: The lease-option lane is replay-safe, receipt-first, and keeps operator tasks and message history consistent without introducing live Supabase wiring.
tasks: Keep the current submit -> booking -> non-booker flow intact while adding receipt-first webhook persistence.
Write and pass failing tests for duplicate Cal.com booking webhooks and duplicate TextGrid inbound events.
Make outbound confirmations and sequence sends append to messages consistently.
Tighten sequence guards so booked, cancelled, opted-out, or manually paused leads do not keep receiving non-booker messages.
Improve inbound SMS resolution in order: provider thread metadata, tenant phone match, ambiguity task.
Run the existing lease-option tests plus the new idempotency coverage before marking the story done.

userStories.title: Add Mission Control surfaces for both lanes without collapsing them together
userStories.goal: Expose lane-separated outbound probate, inbound lease-option, and thin pipeline operator views in the existing Mission Control shell.
userStories.acceptanceGate: One operator can see the outbound probate machine, the inbound lease-option machine, and the minimal downstream pipeline in one cockpit without losing lane separation.
tasks: Extend Mission Control service read models first so API payloads stay lane-separated.
Update the mission-control API contract next with separate outbound probate, inbound lease-option, and opportunity summaries.
Update the frontend last after the payloads are stable.
Keep the UI additive, dense, and explicitly lane-separated instead of merging everything into one list.
Run typecheck, tests, and build after the UI and API changes land.

userStories.title: Add the minimal contract-to-close skeleton
userStories.goal: Create the thin downstream opportunity seam so either lane can open an opportunity without pretending the full title or dispo machine exists.
userStories.acceptanceGate: The MVP can hand a live prospect into a persisted downstream pipeline abstraction without pretending title, TC, and dispo are finished.
tasks: Write and pass failing tests for opportunity creation and stage updates first.
Implement the minimal opportunity model, repository, and service in memory mode.
Wire probate opportunities from positive reply or interested status.
Wire lease-option opportunities from booked and qualified or operator-marked ready states.
Surface only opportunity counts and stage summaries in Mission Control.

userStories.title: Run the branch verification and fixture smoke gates
userStories.goal: Prove the full loose-ends MVP branch is still sane in memory mode with both lane smokes and the required frontend checks.
userStories.acceptanceGate: The repo passes backend tests, Mission Control checks, any required Trigger checks, lane fixture smokes, and still starts in memory mode without live Supabase env requirements for this slice.
tasks: Run backend tests first.
Run Mission Control typecheck, tests, and build second.
Run Trigger checks if new lead-machine tasks were added.
Run one fixture smoke test for lease-option submit -> booking webhook -> non-booker guard.
Run one fixture smoke test for probate intake -> enqueue -> webhook ingest.
Confirm the repo starts in memory mode with no live Supabase env required for this slice.
Update CONTEXT.md, TODO.md, memory.md, and scripts/ralph/progress.txt with the final verified state.
